question: Will NIFC raise the National Preparedness Level to 5 at any point between July 11 and July 24, 2026?

factors.description: Unconditional base rate: only 2 of 11 years (2021, 2024) reached PL5 within the July 11-24 window; 2018 (Jul 27) just missed
Already at PL4 with above-average season (130-137% of 10-yr avg), 44 uncontained large fires, elevated fire weather/heat expanding — resembles the early-escalation years
PL5 requires severe activity across multiple areas simultaneously; only Great Basin & Rocky Mountain at PL4, PNW at PL3, California at PL2-3 in mid-July
PL4-to-PL5 stickiness: several PL4 years (2025, 2022, 2023) never escalated to PL5 at all

summary: NIFC is at PL4 as of July 12, 2026, in an above-average fire season (130-137% of 10-yr average, 44 uncontained large fires, expanding western heat). Historically, PL5 is reached within a July 11-24 window in only ~2 of 11 years (2021, 2024) — an 18% unconditional base rate. Being already at PL4 in an active year with a heat dome pushes above that anchor, but PL5 requires multi-area simultaneous severity and currently only two geographic areas are at PL4, with the Pacific Northwest and California not yet fully engaged (which typically happens late July/August). Net estimate ~22%.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 22% (NO likely)**

NIFC is currently at PL 4 (July 12, 2026) in an above-average fire season. The decisive input was the historical base rate: only 2 of 11 years (2021, 2024) reached PL5 within a July 11-24 window, with 2018 hitting it just after (July 27) and most years not reaching PL5 until August. The active season and expanding heat push the estimate above the 18% unconditional anchor, but PL5 requires broad multi-area engagement that hasn't yet materialized (only Great Basin and Rocky Mountain at PL4; PNW/California still lower), which typically develops in late July/August. Final: **0.22**.
